description: If there exist x and y such that R(x,y), then there exist y and x such that R(x,y).
goal: (-> (exists x (exists y (App (App R x) y))) (exists y (exists x (App (App R x) y))))

hints.text: This is the binary-predicate version of problem 39, with an extra layer of nesting. You need to open two nested [existential-quantifier|existential quantifiers] using `exists-elim`, reorder the witnesses, and close them in reverse order. The proof uses $\exists$-elim twice — once for the outer $\exists x$, once for the inner $\exists y$.
Assume the nested existential: there is some $x$ for which there is some $y$ with $R(x,y)$.
Introduce a fresh [witness|witness] $u$ with `fix`. This will be the witness for the outer $\exists x$.
Assume the body of the outer existential, instantiated with $u$: there exists $y$ such that $R(u,y)$.
Now open the inner existential. Introduce a second fresh witness $v$ for the inner $\exists y$.
Assume $R(u,v)$ — the body of the inner existential, instantiated with $v$.
From $R(u,v)$, use `exists-intro` to replace $u$ with $x$: $\exists x.\; R(x,v)$.
Now use `exists-intro` again to replace $v$ with $y$: $\exists y.\; \exists x.\; R(x,y)$. This is our target conclusion, reached under witnesses $u$, $v$ and assumptions $\exists y.R(u,y)$, $R(u,v)$.

Apply `exists-elim` to close the **inner** witness $v$. It takes the inner existential (step 3), the subproof (step 9), and the witness variable $v$. This discharges $v$ and $R(u,v)$, keeping $\exists y.R(u,y)$.

Apply `exists-elim` again to close the **outer** witness $u$. It takes the outer existential (step 1), the subproof (step 10), and the witness variable $u$. This discharges $u$ and $\exists y.R(u,y)$.
[discharge|Discharge] the original assumption to finish the [implication|implication].
